description_long: This object is a vintage Underwood typewriter, featuring a robust black metal frame with intricate mechanical components visible throughout its structure. The front showcases a full set of round, raised keys with metallic rims and white lettering, arranged in a classic typewriter layout. Above the keys, the typebars and ribbon spools are exposed, hinting at the complex internal mechanisms that drive the typing action. The rear of the typewriter displays the carriage and roller, with a long metal bar for paper alignment and movement. Both sides reveal gears, levers, and additional mechanical details, emphasizing the industrial design typical of early 20th-century office equipment. The overall appearance is utilitarian yet elegant, with a matte black finish and subtle metallic highlights.
description: A vintage Underwood typewriter with a black metal frame, round white-labeled keys, and exposed mechanical components. Its design is industrial and detailed, reflecting early 20th-century craftsmanship.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full keyboard with round keys, typebars, and the Underwood logo above the keys.
description_view_1: Left side view revealing the depth of the frame, gears, and levers, with the keyboard profile visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the carriage, roller, and long metal bar for paper movement, with the internal structure partially visible.
description_view_3: Right side view highlighting additional gears and levers, and the side profile of the keyboard and frame.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
